content.problemStatement:
You are given 444 integers X,L,R,X, L, R, X,L,R, and MMM.

Consider the sequence of integers AAA of length R‚àíL+1R-L+1R‚àíL+1, defined by

Ai=(X‚äï(L+i‚àí1))‚Äämod‚ÄäMA_i = (X \oplus (L + i - 1)) \bmod MAi‚Äã=(X‚äï(L+i‚àí1))modMwhere where ‚äï\oplus‚äï is the Bitwise xor operator.

That is, the iii-th element of the sequence is obtained by taking the result of the bitwise XOR of XXX and (L+i‚àí1)(L + i - 1)(L+i‚àí1), and computing its remainder modulo MMM.

So, the sequence is:

[(X‚äïL)‚Äämod‚ÄäM,(X‚äï(L+1))‚Äämod‚ÄäM,‚Ä¶,(X‚äïR)‚Äämod‚ÄäM][ (X\oplus L)\bmod M, (X\oplus(L+1))\bmod M, \ldots, (X\oplus R)\bmod M ][(X‚äïL)modM,(X‚äï(L+1))modM,‚Ä¶,(X‚äïR)modM]Your task is to find the maximum frequency of a number in this sequence.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first and only line of each test case contains 444 space-separated integers X,L,R,MX,L,R,MX,L,R,M, as described in the statement.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the maximum frequency of a number in the described sequence.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111:

The sequence is [6‚Äämod‚Ää5,7‚Äämod‚Ää5,4‚Äämod‚Ää5,5‚Äämod‚Ää5,10‚Äämod‚Ää5,11‚Äämod‚Ää5,8‚Äämod‚Ää5][ 6 \bmod 5, 7 \bmod 5, 4 \bmod 5, 5 \bmod 5, 10 \bmod 5, 11 \bmod 5, 8 \bmod 5 ][6mod5,7mod5,4mod5,5mod5,10mod5,11mod5,8mod5] = [1,2,4,0,0,1,3][ 1, 2, 4, 0, 0, 1, 3 ][1,2,4,0,0,1,3].
The maximum frequency is 222 (obtained by the values 000 and 111).
